body_paragraphs: The month Miami spent at the center of the world ends this afternoon. The final kicks off at 3, and if you're not at Hard Rock you have three free county parks and every screen in Wynwood to choose from. Enjoy it; the city won't sound like this again for a while.

The quieter story is the week ahead. The Fed meets in ten days with hike odds near a coin flip, Warsh has stopped telling markets what he'll do, and Friday's tape suggests positioning has started without him: chips sold hard, the Nasdaq gave up nearly three percent on the week. Alphabet, Intel and Tesla all report into that nervousness. If rates and the AI trade are going to collide, this is the week it happens.
The Marlins picked a bad weekend to go cold. Swept in Milwaukee, cushion thinning, Houston next. Baseball's cruelty is that a 20-6 June buys you exactly nothing in July.

The rabbit hole: why Stoicism keeps returning

public_day_context.weather: 90 / 80, strong UV, wet-season afternoon storms possible
public_day_context.public_event: World Cup final, 3pm - free watch parties at Tropical, Amelia Earhart and Palmetto parks

what_changed.title: Marlins swept out of the break
what_changed.previous_state: Hit the All-Star break 52-45, holding the wild card pole position after a franchise-record June.
what_changed.current_state: Swept in Milwaukee - 2-1 in ten innings, then 8-6 twice. Three straight losses, Houston series next.
what_changed.why_it_matters: The cushion is real but thinning; the Houston series now carries weight it should not have needed to.

what_changed.title: Fed week arrives with positioning already visible
what_changed.previous_state: Hike odds were rising through last week; Warsh hawkish, no forward guidance.
what_changed.current_state: Meeting is 10 days out, odds near a coin flip, and Friday's chip-led selloff (Nasdaq -2.9% on the week) suggests markets are positioning early.
what_changed.why_it_matters: A hike repricing hits rate-sensitive assets first - the tape is already telling you where the nerves are.

what_changed.title: The World Cup ends today
what_changed.previous_state: Miami hosted through the knockouts; bronze final played at Hard Rock yesterday.
what_changed.current_state: Final kicks off at 3pm. Miami's hosting role concludes with county-wide watch parties.
what_changed.why_it_matters: Last day to be part of it - and the city gets its summer back tomorrow.

what_changed.title: Dolphins camp opens tomorrow
what_changed.previous_state: Camp schedule announced; Hafley's first season taking shape on paper.
what_changed.current_state: Rookies report Monday to Baptist Health; veterans follow July 28.
what_changed.why_it_matters: The rebuild stops being theoretical this week.

themes.title: Fed week: the hike question is live
themes.thesis: For the first time in this cycle, a July hike is a genuine coin flip.
themes.synthesis: The FOMC meets July 28-29 with FedWatch odds near 46.5% and a chair who has deliberately stopped offering guidance. Inflation is the reason: CPI at 4.2%, the hottest since 2023. With no signal from Warsh, every data print this week does the talking.
themes.new_today: Fed week begins with positioning already visible in Friday's selloff.
themes.why_it_matters: Your world reprices off this meeting - debt costs, cap rates, and the second half of the year.

sources.headline: A July rate hike? The odds are rising

themes.title: The AI trade meets a rate wall
themes.thesis: This week tests whether the AI trade can absorb higher-for-longer rates.
themes.synthesis: Alphabet, Intel, Tesla and GE Vernova all report during Fed week, right after semiconductors led the market lower. The Nasdaq gave up nearly 3% last week. PMIs and new home sales fill out the calendar - a full stress test of growth assets against a hawkish backdrop.
themes.new_today: Friday closed with chips leading the decline; earnings begin midweek.
themes.why_it_matters: If the AI complex cracks under rate pressure, it moves everything - including real asset allocations.

sources.headline: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq post weekly losses as semiconductors get smoked

themes.title: The last day of Miami's World Cup
themes.thesis: A month of hosting ends at 3pm - go be part of the ending.
themes.synthesis: The final plays this afternoon, and the county built one last day around it: free watch parties at Tropical, Amelia Earhart and Palmetto parks from 2pm, a big screen at the Miami Beach Bandshell, and Grails running it across 75 TVs with no cover. Tomorrow the city exhales.
themes.new_today: Final day - everything above is today only.
themes.why_it_matters: These collective-city moments are rare; this one closes this afternoon.

sources.headline: Free World Cup watch parties around Miami
Final watch parties at three county parks

themes.title: The Marlins' cushion thins
themes.thesis: Three losses in Milwaukee turned a comfortable wild card lead into a real race.
themes.synthesis: The sweep stings for its shape as much as its substance: an extra-innings one-run loss, then two games the bullpen could not hold. The 20-6 June still matters - it built the cushion now being spent - but the Houston series ahead is suddenly consequential.
themes.why_it_matters: The first real pennant-race baseball in Miami in years is happening now, not in September.

sources.headline: All-Star week came at the perfect time

themes.title: Three franchises, three clocks
themes.thesis: Miami's teams are running on deliberately different timelines - and that's the story.
themes.synthesis: The Heat traded for Giannis and are monitoring Klay Thompson. The Panthers rebuilt their roster in eleven days around Tkachuk. The Dolphins, meanwhile, are asking for patience: rookies report tomorrow to a first-year coach with a young roster. Win-now, win-again, and wait - all in one market.
themes.new_today: Dolphins rookies report tomorrow; camp battles start taking shape.
themes.why_it_matters: Knowing which clock each team is on tells you how to watch the next twelve months.

sources.headline: Heat, Panthers went big, but Dolphins are asking for patience

themes.title: The summer table
themes.thesis: The city's eating map is being redrawn while everyone watches soccer.
themes.synthesis: La Sponda opens on Grove Isle with bay views, a Michelin-trained Greek arrives at Manoli in the Grove, PopUp Bagels lands in Brickell, and China Grill returns to South Beach after a decade. Meanwhile it is peak mango season everywhere - the county's best seasonal excuse to eat well.
themes.new_today: Nothing new today - this is the standing map for the next month of dinners.
themes.why_it_matters: Openings this dense come once a summer; the good tables will be gone by September.

themes.title: Why the Stoa keeps returning
themes.thesis: Stoicism's revival is less a trend than a recurring answer to loud decades.
themes.synthesis: Philosophy Now devotes a full issue to Stoicism's place in history and modern life, while USC's writeup traces why a 2,300-year-old system keeps finding new footholds: it treats attention as the scarce resource and offers drills, not slogans. Useful framing for a week when the news will try to spend your attention for you.
themes.new_today: A quality cluster of serious writing, versus the usual listicle Stoicism.
themes.why_it_matters: This is your operating philosophy getting proper intellectual treatment.

sources.headline: Stoicism in History and Modern Life (Issue 157)

rabbit_hole.title: Stoicism in History and Modern Life
rabbit_hole.creator: Philosophy Now, Issue 157
rabbit_hole.why: A full issue of serious philosophy writing on why the Stoa endures - history, criticism, and practice, not quotes on sunsets. The right companion for a week that will test the discipline of attention.

beautiful_thing.title: Miami Marine Stadium
beautiful_thing.creator: Hilario Candela
beautiful_thing.body: A 27-year-old Cuban-born architect designed this concrete grandstand on Virginia Key with a folded-plate roof that cantilevers 65 feet over the seats - no columns, just origami in concrete holding itself above the water. It hosted powerboat races and Sunday concerts until Hurricane Andrew closed it in 1992, and it has stood since as Miami's most beautiful ruin: graffitied, salt-worn, and still structurally daring by any era's standard. Preservationists have fought for decades to bring it back. Look at what a young immigrant architect thought Miami deserved.

noticing_prompt.text: Wherever the final has you this afternoon, spend one minute watching the crowd instead of the screen. Collective attention is its own spectacle.

evening_quote.text: Nowhere can man find a quieter or more untroubled retreat than in his own soul.

evening_unwind.evening_prompt: What felt urgent today that, by tonight, already does not?

quote.text: We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.
